title: rhodanthe chlorocephala subsp. splendida
intro: Rhodanthe chlorocephala subsp. splendida, also known as the Billy buttons or Woolly heads, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and antioxidant effects, providing various health benefits such as improved immune function and reduced inflammation. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Rhodanthe chlorocephala subsp. splendida can be infused as a tea or used topically as a poultice to harness its medicinal properties.
